description_long: This object is a set of red, cylindrical LPG (liquefied petroleum gas) cylinders, commonly used for domestic cooking gas storage in India. The tanks are made of metal, with a rounded top and bottom, and feature a protective ring structure around the valve at the top. The surface is painted a matte reddish-brown, with yellow Devanagari script reading 'Bharat Gas' on the side, indicating the brand. Each cylinder has a brass valve at the top for gas release and filling, and a sturdy base ring for stability. The tanks are grouped together, with one lying horizontally, showing the flat, circular base and the handle structure. These cylinders are typically found in urban residential or commercial settings, such as kitchens, storage rooms, or delivery areas.
description: A group of red, cylindrical LPG gas cylinders with protective top rings, yellow branding, and brass valves, commonly used for domestic gas storage in India.
description_view_0: Three upright gas cylinders and one lying horizontally, showing the protective top rings and yellow branding.
description_view_1: Four cylinders, with the horizontal one in the foreground, clearly displaying the base and handle structure.
description_view_2: Rear view of the group, highlighting the rounded tops, protective rings, and the brass valves.
description_view_3: Three upright cylinders viewed from the back, with the branding partially visible and the top rings prominent.
